original_abstract: Abstract This chapter provides an analysis into the factors that impact the effective adoption and successful implementation of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies in Africa. The study begins by defining the concept of “responsible AI” and what this means specifically for technologies developed and used within Africa. Further sections within the chapter present challenges including digital literacy, lack of local AI talent and governmental barriers that impede. The chapter also goes into an in-depth analysis of the AI startup and research landscape within the African continent, highlighting organisations of interest and concerning trends. ‘Challenges to effective AI adoption and Implementation in Africa’ concludes by envisioning what responsible AI could look like in the African context and provides actionable recommendations for making strides towards this goal.
humanized_abstract: Abstract This chapter examines the variables affecting the adoption and implementation of artificial intelligence (AI) in Africa. This chapter introduces the concept of the term “Responsible AI” with respect to the African context. This chapter further elaborates the effects of low literacy, lack of sufficient skills in the field of AI in Africa, as well as certain obstacles that can hinder the implementation of AI technologies. This chapter also focuses on the ecosystem of the start-ups and the research institutions in the field of AI within the African region. It highlights the significant projects that are worth mentioning, as well as the challenges faced by the key players in this sector and trends that need to be addressed. “Challenges to effective AI adoption and Implementation in Africa” culminates by explaining the concept of the term “Responsible AI” within the context of Africa and includes several recommendations on how to achieve such a goal.
